page: Microsoft Azure E32s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the ESv4 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 virtual CPUs mapped to 16 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The CPU operates at a base frequency of 2.6 GHz and includes a 35 MB L3 cache. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. No local storage is bundled with this instance, though it is Premium Storage capable, and it does not include any GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

Performance benchmarks place the E32s_v4 consistently in the average tier across most workloads. The processor achieved a single-core stress-ng score of 1412.79 and a multi-core score of 29817.36, while Geekbench returned a single-core score of 1332.0 and a multi-core score of 10843.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 1663.4 GB/sec, dropping to 90.27 GB/sec for large uncached blocks, and a write bandwidth of 43.64 GB/sec. Database and memory-intensive operations are supported by a PassMark database score of 10604.21 and a Redis SET throughput of 26.54 million operations per second.

The lack of local storage requires reliance on external premium storage volumes, which introduces network-based storage latency tradeoffs. Additionally, while the instance handles smaller language model inference adequately, achieving 47.23 tokens per second for prompt processing on a 7B model, it exhibits poor performance on larger models, such as 4.56 tokens per second for a 70B model, and lacks GPU acceleration. Given its high memory density and solid database benchmark results, the E32s_v4 is best suited for memory-intensive workloads, relational databases, in-memory caching systems like Redis, and enterprise applications requiring substantial RAM without intensive GPU requirements.
description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the ESv4 family, powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this x86_64 instance provides 32 vCPUs (16 physical cores) running at 2.6 GHz, paired with 256.0 GB of RAM. It features no local storage, relying instead on its Premium Storage capability, and lacks GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place the server in the average tier, with a multi-core stress-ng score of 29817.36 and a Redis SET throughput of 26.54 million operations per second. Large-block memory write bandwidth is measured at 43.64 GB/sec. Due to its high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core and strong database benchmark performance, the E32s_v4 is highly suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, and in-memory caching workloads.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v4 is a memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive databases and enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32s_v4 is a memory-optimized VM with 32 vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, ideal for databases and caching.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring 256 GB RAM and 32 vCPUs, designed for high-capacity database and caching workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8272CL processor with 32 vCPUs and 16 physical cores
256.0 GB of system memory providing a high 8.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Premium Storage capable configuration with no bundled local storage
Average multi-core performance with 29817.36 stress-ng score
Proven database capability with 26.54 million Redis SET operations per second
Best suited for relational databases, in-memory caching, and memory-intensive enterprise apps
